schema.appName: Post Office RD Calculator — Recurring Deposit
schema.appDescription: Calculate Post Office Recurring Deposit maturity. Current rate 6.7% p.a. compounded quarterly. Minimum ₹100/month. 5-year fixed tenure.

howToSteps.name: Enter your details
howToSteps.text: Fill in the input fields for the Post Office RD Calculator. All inputs have smart defaults.

howToSteps.name: View instant results
howToSteps.text: Results update in real time as you type — no submit button needed.

howToSteps.name: Explore breakdown
howToSteps.text: Open the year-by-year breakdown table to see how your numbers change over time.

howToSteps.name: Compare scenarios
howToSteps.text: Adjust the inputs to compare different scenarios and find the best option.

faq.q: What is Post Office RD and its current rate?
faq.a: Post Office Recurring Deposit is a 5-year scheme with a current rate of 6.7% p.a. (Q1 FY 2026-27), compounded quarterly. Minimum ₹100/month. Unlike bank RDs, there is no TDS on PO RD interest. It has sovereign guarantee (Government of India backed).

faq.q: Can PO RD be extended after 5 years?
faq.a: Yes. PO RD can be extended for another 5 years. Deposits can also be stopped after 3 years (treated as closed early). Premature closure allowed after 3 years at applicable simple interest rate.

faq.q: How does PO RD compare to bank RD?
faq.a: PO RD (6.7%) vs SBI RD (6.5%) — PO RD has slightly higher rate with sovereign guarantee. Bank RDs may offer up to 7.5% at small finance banks with higher rates but less security. No TDS on PO RD is an additional advantage.

faq.q: Is the Post Office RD Calculator free?
faq.a: Yes, the Post Office RD Calculator on CalcPhi is completely free with no registration required.
No. All calculations run in your browser. We do not store any financial data.

faq.q: Can I use the Post Office RD Calculator on mobile?
faq.a: Yes. CalcPhi is fully mobile-optimised and works on all modern smartphones.

examples.heading: ₹5,000/month PO RD for 5 years
examples.text: ₹5,000/month in PO RD at 6.7% p.a. for 5 years: total invested = ₹3,00,000. Maturity = approximately ₹3,54,000. Interest earned = ₹54,000.

examples.heading: ₹10,000/month — 5-year wealth building
examples.text: ₹10,000/month at 6.7% over 5 years builds a corpus of ₹7,08,000 on ₹6,00,000 invested. Post office RD is ideal for disciplined monthly savings with government backing.
